assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md states: 'The vulnerable library is **openssl-libs**, which is a system-level RPM package (not a Cargo crate, npm package, or Go module). The ecosystem mappings in the 2.2.x stream's `security-matrix.md` include an RPM ecosystem entry with: Lock File: rpms.lock.yaml'. Concludes '**Ecosystem: RPM** (system package).' The detection is based on the library name and ecosystem mappings configuration, correctly identifying RPM rather than Cargo.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md section 2.1 lists all five 2.2.x versions (2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4) in the supportability matrix. Section 2.3 uses rpms.lock.yaml with check command 'git show <tag>:rpms.lock.yaml | grep openssl-libs' and shows extracted openssl-libs versions for each: 2.2.0=3.0.7-25.el9_3, 2.2.1=3.0.7-27.el9_4, 2.2.2=retag, 2.2.3=3.0.7-28.el9_4, 2.2.4=3.0.7-28.el9_4. The summary table (section 2.4) also includes all five versions.

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context classifies the package origin as explicit install because openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml, not inherited from the base image (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md section 2.3.5 states: 'rpms.lock.yaml: **present** -> explicit install' and 'Origin: explicit install (openssl-libs specified in rpms.in.yaml / rpms.lock.yaml)'. It further clarifies: 'The package is directly managed in the Konflux release repo lock file, not inherited from a base image.'

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md states: '**RPM** (system package). Single task -- the fix happens directly in the Konflux release repo. No upstream backport step needed.' Only one jira.create_issue call is shown (lines 30-36), creating a single Task. No upstream backport or downstream propagation tasks are created. The task targets the Konflux release repo (rhtpa-release.0.4.z).

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task Description contains all required sections: '## Repository' (rhtpa-release.0.4.z), '## Target Branch' (main), '## Description' (remediation details with affected versions and advisory links), '## Implementation Notes' (update instructions for rpms.in.yaml/rpms.lock.yaml), '## Acceptance Criteria' (two checklist items). There is no '## Files to Modify' section, which is correct per remediation-templates.md: 'Files to Modify and Files to Create are intentionally omitted.'

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context includes an SBOM verification line — either showing the cosign comparison result or noting that SBOM verification was skipped because cosign is not available (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md section 2.3.5 (Dependency Chain Context) contains only: rpms.lock.yaml presence check, origin classification (explicit install), remediation guidance, and a note about the package being directly managed. There is no mention of SBOM, cosign, SBOM verification, or any statement that SBOM verification was skipped. The section completely omits the optional SBOM verification step.
